stop_reason: The first arm exceeded both the registered token and cost ceilings. The preregistered stopping rule prohibited rerun, substitution, remaining arms, and judging.
learning: Native per-tool output retention limits reduced tool payload size but did not prevent the model-visible base context from recurring across tool turns. The arm cost estimator treated the base prompt as one-time input and therefore undercounted repeated and cached input. A successor must price every model turn or use a preregistered one-inference design.
claim: V31 provides no comparative evidence and does not support or refute the C3 lift hypothesis.
